name: B7-33
subtitle: Relaxin-2 Analog | Anti-Fibrotic & Cardiovascular

modifications: Derived from the B-chain of human relaxin-2
Simplified single-chain structure vs. native two-chain relaxin-2
Retains RXFP1 receptor binding and activation

overview: B7-33 is a single-chain peptide analog of human relaxin-2 that selectively activates the relaxin family peptide receptor 1 (RXFP1). Unlike native relaxin-2, which requires a complex two-chain A/B structure connected by disulfide bonds, B7-33 achieves RXFP1 activation with a much simpler single-chain design. This makes it significantly easier and more cost-effective to synthesize. Preclinical research demonstrates potent anti-fibrotic, vasodilatory, and cardioprotective properties, positioning B7-33 as a promising therapeutic candidate for fibrotic diseases, heart failure, and vascular dysfunction.
mechanism: Selectively activates RXFP1, the primary receptor for relaxin-2, triggering downstream signaling cascades that inhibit fibroblast activation and collagen deposition, promote extracellular matrix remodeling via increased matrix metalloproteinase (MMP) activity, enhance nitric oxide-mediated vasodilation, and reduce inflammatory cytokine expression. B7-33 appears to preferentially engage pERK1/2 signaling pathways while showing reduced cAMP activation compared to native relaxin-2, suggesting biased agonism at RXFP1.
keyBenefits: Potent anti-fibrotic activity across multiple organ systems
Cardioprotective effects and potential to attenuate cardiac remodeling
Much simpler to synthesize than native two-chain relaxin-2
Selective RXFP1 agonism with biased signaling profile
Preclinical efficacy in models of heart failure and fibrosis

items.name: Cardiac Fibrosis
items.description: Reduces myocardial fibrosis and collagen deposition, attenuating adverse cardiac remodeling in preclinical heart failure models.

items.name: Vasodilation
items.description: Enhances nitric oxide-mediated vasodilation, reducing vascular resistance and improving blood flow in preclinical studies.

items.name: Heart Failure
items.description: Demonstrates cardioprotective effects in animal models of heart failure, improving cardiac function and reducing fibrotic burden.

items.name: Organ Fibrosis
items.description: Inhibits fibroblast differentiation into myofibroblasts and reduces extracellular matrix deposition in multiple organ systems.

items.name: Renal Fibrosis
items.description: Shows protective effects against kidney fibrosis progression in preclinical disease models.

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous injection is the primary route studied in preclinical research. Dosing protocols are based on animal model data and have not been validated in human trials.

steps: Clean work area and hands thoroughly
Calculate required BAC water volume using calculator
Inject slowly down vial side (not directly onto powder)
Store in refrigerator, use within 28 days

keyBenefits: Primary route used in preclinical research
Rotate injection sites to prevent irritation

interactions.notes: Potential synergistic effects for cardiac protection; both target complementary pathways involved in cardiac remodeling and fibrosis.
No known negative interactions; different mechanisms of action with complementary tissue-repair and anti-fibrotic properties.

timeline.effects: Onset of vasodilatory effects; initial anti-fibrotic signaling activation
Progressive reduction in fibrotic markers based on preclinical timelines
Measurable improvements in tissue fibrosis and cardiovascular parameters in animal models

common: Potential transient hypotension due to vasodilatory effects
stopSigns: Persistent or symptomatic hypotension (dizziness, lightheadedness, fainting)
Severe injection site reactions or signs of infection
contraindications: Pre-existing hypotension or conditions exacerbated by vasodilation
Pregnancy or breastfeeding (no safety data available)
Concurrent use of potent antihypertensive agents without medical supervision
No human safety data exists -- all protocols are extrapolated from preclinical research

good: Clear solution after reconstitution with no particles
warning: Slight clumping that dissolves with gentle swirling (may occur from shipping)
bad: Discolored or wet powder indicating degradation
Cloudy solution, visible particles, or precipitates after reconstitution

references.title: A single-chain peptide derived from the relaxin B-chain selectively activates RXFP1
references.keyFindings: Identified B7-33 as a single-chain relaxin analog that selectively activates RXFP1, demonstrating that a simplified single-chain peptide can replicate key signaling functions of native two-chain relaxin-2.

references.title: The single-chain relaxin mimetic, B7-33, maintains anti-fibrotic activity in liver and kidney fibrosis models
references.keyFindings: B7-33 reduced fibrosis markers in both liver and kidney fibrosis models, demonstrating organ-protective anti-fibrotic effects comparable to native relaxin-2 despite its simplified structure.

references.title: The relaxin receptor as a therapeutic target -- perspectives from evolution and drug targeting
references.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of RXFP1 as a drug target, discussing relaxin-2 and analogs including B7-33 for cardiovascular, fibrotic, and reproductive indications.

references.title: B7-33 replicates the vasoprotective effects of relaxin in mouse models of cardiovascular disease
references.journal: Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences
references.keyFindings: B7-33 replicated the vasoprotective and anti-fibrotic effects of native relaxin in mouse models, reducing vascular stiffness and improving cardiovascular outcomes.

references.title: The role of relaxin and its receptor (RXFP1) in the pathogenesis and treatment of fibrotic diseases
references.keyFindings: Reviews the anti-fibrotic mechanisms of relaxin/RXFP1 signaling, including inhibition of TGF-beta-driven fibroblast activation and collagen synthesis, with implications for simplified analogs like B7-33.

quickStats.storage: Lyophilized: freezer long-term. Reconstituted: 2-8 C for up to 28 days
